message.content:
<system-reminder>
The user named this session "laboratory-v2". This may indicate the session's focus or intent.
</system-reminder>

I am pasting a handoff doc from the previous session below. One thing to call out first, though... that information frames the implementation and research wrok as some we would commission. That is not true--the laboratory plugin currently lives in the sanctum, /workspace/nexus-mk2. It is not part of the nexus framework. YOu and will be implementing this functionality directly.

So: commission the framework-verification first — it's the unblock. The archive design conversation can happen in
  parallel. Once both land, the rest of the children are commissionable.                                                
                                                                  
  Handoff prompt                                                                                                        
                                                                  
  Resuming the Laboratory apparatus work from the previous session.
                                                                                                                        
  Context:
  The previous session designed an MVP0 for a Laboratory apparatus — a guild-side                                       
  toolkit for running experiments on guild configurations (cost/quality tuning for                                      
  nexus dev; prompt/plugin evaluation for end users). The design lives in the click                                     
  tree under c-mom18vmh; the implementation companion is c-moma9llq with 14                                             
  commissionable children.                                                                                              
                                                                                                                        
  Read first:                                                                                                           
  - nsg click extract --id c-mom18vmh         # full design + implementation tree
  - experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-artifacts.md                                                                   
                                              # the standalone-bash spec the apparatus                                  
                                              # supersedes; its run.yaml format is the                                  
                                              # template for the manifest / ext schema                                  
  - previous session transcript                # session id 905f54f8-fd5c-49df-9294-b856202a7740                        
                                                                                                                        
  What's decided (eight concluded sub-clicks under c-mom18vmh):                                                         
  - Name: Laboratory (kept; old data-mirroring stub was Sean+Coco internal)                                             
  - Writ type: 'trial' (singular concept; 'experiment' as grouping is v2/parked)                                        
  - Body = prose; ext.laboratory.config = structured config                                                             
  - Authoring: YAML manifest via 'nsg lab trial post --manifest <file>'                                                 
  - Scenario IS a rig template (no separate primitive); one canonical                                                   
    post-and-collect-default template; extension via plugin contribution, not                                           
    in-template slots                                                                                                   
  - Fixtures (setup/teardown w/ dep DAG) decoupled from probes (data extraction)                                        
  - Fixture scope: trial-only in v1; hooks for v2 (scope field, mutability flag)                                        
  - Cross-guild action: shell-out via 'nsg --guild-root <other>'; real engine                                           
    surface deferred (parked at c-mom9vm3n)                       
  - Data home: lab-host guild's books; sanctum still holds methodology                                                  
                                                                                                                        
  Two things to resolve before broad commissioning:                                                                     
  1. Archive output target (click c-momaa5o9, design + impl bundled). Pick: lab                                         
     books, sanctum mirror, or hybrid + per-trial storage layout + trial→archive                                        
     linkage. Discuss with Sean.                                                                                        
  2. Framework prerequisites (click c-moma9psb) — first commission to dispatch.                                         
     Verify: does 'nsg plugin install' accept git SHAs? does the rig framework                                          
     support non-anima (shell/script) engines? does Reckoner accept writ-type                                           
     priority registration from kit contributions? gh org permissions for codex                                         
     repo create/delete? The non-anima-engines question is the load-bearing one;                                        
     if the framework doesn't support it, the apparatus design needs adjustment.                                        
                                                                                                                        
  Suggested first move:                                                                                                 
  Draft a brief for the framework-verification commission (c-moma9psb). It's a                                          
  discovery commission — output is a doc enumerating actual command surfaces and                                        
  flagging gaps. Its results unblock the other 13 children. The archive design                                          
  conversation can run in parallel with Sean.                                                                           
                                                                                                                        
  After verification lands, the natural commissioning order is:                                                         
  1. Plugin reshape (c-moma9qyz) — foundation                                                                           
  2. Trial writ type + schema (c-moma9sg5)                                                                              
  3. Manifest CLI (c-moma9ty6)                                                                                          
  4. Rig template (c-moma9vrm)                                                                                          
  5-9. Engines (parallelizable: codex/guild fixtures, scenario, probes)                                                 
  10. Archive (after design resolves)                                                                                   
  11. Smoke test                                                                                                        
  12-13. Retire infra spec + documentation                                                                              
  14. Port first real trial (capstone)                                                                                  
                                                                  
  Key click IDs:                                                                                                        
  - Top-level apparatus design:  c-mom18vmh
  - Implementation parent:        c-moma9llq                                                                            
  - Framework verification:       c-moma9psb (first dispatch)                                                           
  - Archive (open design):        c-momaa5o9
  - Spec being superseded:        c-molveg89                                                                            
                                                                                                                        
  That should be enough to resume cold. Want me to drop it into .scratch/ as a file, or is in-chat enough?

content.content:
# c-mom18vmh-c1260c725e9f [live]
> Build the Laboratory apparatus — a general-purpose toolkit for running experiments on guild configurations. Audiences: nexus development (cost/quality tuning) and end users (evaluating their own prompts/plugins/configs). Architecture: experiment as first-class research unit; trial-runs as writ-shaped execution units; fixtures (setup/teardown with a dep DAG) decoupled from probes (data extraction); declarative scenarios + end-conditions; per-experiment analysis instruments. Lab runs in a 'real' guild that owns all experiment data; temp test-guilds are pure observation surfaces. Generalizes the P3 infrastructure spec (c-molveg89) into a reusable apparatus. Reuses the 'Laboratory' name (data-mirroring stub retired 2026-04-30 is internal-only).
Status: live
Created: 2026-04-30T22:05:35.898Z

## c-mom18yxq-7a21044d2c02 [concluded]
> Is 'experiment' a writ type, or do experiment-and-trial split into separate concepts?
Status: concluded
Conclusion: Split: experiment is the higher-level research unit (design + verdict); a trial-run is the writ-shaped execution unit (one apparatus instantiation, one scenario, one capture). One experiment fans out to N trial writs. Conflating them would mash 'apparatus completed' with 'hypothesis confirmed' (a successful experiment can reject its hypothesis). Trial writ name still TBD — child click.
Created: 2026-04-30T22:05:40.190Z
Resolved: 2026-04-30T22:05:54.414Z

### c-mom19i4c-d8f6ff956216 [concluded]
> What's the writ-type name for one trial of an experiment? Bare 'run' is generic; want experiment-flavored (e.g., 'trial', 'assay', 'sortie', 'session-of-trial', etc.).
Status: concluded
Conclusion: Trial. Singular concept for MVP0 — one trial writ = one apparatus execution. 'Experiment' reserved for a future grouping concept (parent over multiple trials), introduced additively when comparative-analysis features earn their keep. Plain English across domains (drug trials, beta trials, trial runs); reads naturally for both A/B-test and research-style users.
Created: 2026-04-30T22:06:05.052Z
Resolved: 2026-05-01T02:07:07.196Z

## c-mom18zkt-9a0653e77a86 [concluded]
> Where do experiment-generated data live — in temp/test guilds, sanctum, or the lab-host guild?
Status: concluded
Conclusion: All experiment-generated data lives in the 'real' guild that hosts the Laboratory apparatus. Temp test-guilds are pure observation surfaces — set up, poked at, dumped, destroyed — never authoritative storage. Sanctum still holds methodology/inputs/exports for the research record; the lab-guild's books are the runtime source of truth.
Created: 2026-04-30T22:05:41.021Z
Resolved: 2026-04-30T22:05:57.857Z

## c-mom1907v-132b771959c7 [concluded]
> Reuse the 'Laboratory' name for the new apparatus, or pick a fresh one to avoid confusion with the retired data-mirroring plugin?
Status: concluded
Conclusion: Keep 'Laboratory.' The retired plugin's meaning was internal to Sean+Coco — no users carry mental models of it. The name is the clearest signal of intent for future users of the apparatus.
Created: 2026-04-30T22:05:41.851Z
Resolved: 2026-04-30T22:05:59.717Z

## c-mom19117-73866ec9da2b [concluded]
> Decompose 'fixture' into Fixture (lifecycle: setup/teardown) + Probe (data extraction), or keep the operations fused on a single concept?
Status: concluded
Conclusion: Split. Fixtures handle setup/teardown with an explicit dep DAG; probes extract data from one or more fixtures and run independently of the fixture lifecycle. Enables multi-probe-per-fixture (mid-trial diagnostic dump + end-of-trial archive dump), probe reuse across fixture types, and zero-probe fixtures (nexus-branch is one).
Created: 2026-04-30T22:05:42.907Z
Resolved: 2026-04-30T22:06:02.887Z

## c-mom19282-02f03d14a178 [concluded]
> Fixture scope/lifetime — trial-scoped (fresh per trial) vs experiment-scoped (shared across trials) vs ad-hoc? How is scope declared and enforced, and does it interact with the dep DAG (e.g., a trial-scoped fixture depending on an experiment-scoped one)?
Status: concluded
Conclusion: MVP0 ships trial-scope only — every fixture is fresh per trial, torn down at trial end. Reserve hooks for v2 without implementing: 'scope' field on fixture declarations (only 'trial' valid in v1), 'mutability' field ('mutable | read-only | snapshotted', informational in v1), DAG validator stub aware of 'lab > experiment > trial' ordering. Validation rule for the DAG: scope(B) >= scope(A) for every edge A->B (a dependency must outlive its dependent). Defer experiment- and lab-scope until a motivating use case appears (likely P3 with 20+ trials sharing a codex base SHA where re-cloning dominates cost).
Created: 2026-04-30T22:05:44.450Z
Resolved: 2026-05-01T02:12:22.861Z

## c-mom193ka-9aaac76e0e97 [concluded]
> How do we declaratively express the scenario — the workload that runs inside a trial after setup (commissions to post, ordering, end-conditions like queue-drains/timeout/rig-completes)? The spec doc currently assumes a human posts commissions manually; a real toolkit needs a scenario primitive.
Status: concluded
Conclusion: Scenario IS a rig template, dispatched via existing Spider/Reckoner machinery. No separate scenario primitive. MVP0 ships one canonical template (post-and-collect-default) covering the common shape (post N briefs sequentially, wait for terminal, run standard probes, archive). No in-template extension mechanism (no slots/graft); extension is via plugin-contributed templates. Friction-by-default avoids template proliferation; slot mechanics enter only when 3+ templates share most structure.
Created: 2026-04-30T22:05:46.186Z
Resolved: 2026-05-01T02:07:10.894Z

## c-mom9vkls-d395c6a934d1 [concluded]
> How is trial config carried into the rig — writ body, ext, or both?
Status: concluded
Conclusion: ext.laboratory.config carries the structured trial config (fixtures, plugin pins, files, brief refs, probe selections). Body stays free-form prose for human description (variant intent, hypothesis, notes). Authoring surface is a YAML manifest (same shape as existing run.yaml in experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-artifacts.md); CLI 'nsg lab trial post --manifest <file>' validates against the laboratory schema and posts the writ. Manifest format and writ ext are the same shape with a thin CLI translation — collapses two formats into one schema.
Created: 2026-05-01T02:07:11.632Z
Resolved: 2026-05-01T02:07:21.129Z

## c-mom9vm3n-3f27aefd4288 [parked]
> Cross-guild engine surface — when does shell-out via 'nsg --guild-root <other>' stop being good enough? MVP path is CLI shell-out; track for symptoms (auth, perf, observability across the boundary) that motivate a real cross-guild engine surface.
Status: parked
Created: 2026-05-01T02:07:13.571Z

## c-moma9llq-195259699140 [live]
> Implementation — turn the Laboratory MVP0 design into a working apparatus. Children decompose the build into commissionable units; each child becomes a brief + posted commission when ready to dispatch. First few items (framework verification, plugin reshape, schema/CLI) unblock everything else; engines and archive can parallelize once the skeleton lands.
Status: live
Created: 2026-05-01T02:18:06.110Z

### c-moma9psb-9d38a7ea4858 [live]
> Verify framework prerequisites — confirm 'nsg init', 'nsg plugin install' (does it accept git SHAs / refs, not just npm semver?), and 'nsg codex add' invocation surfaces against current framework source. Confirm gh org permissions for codex repo create/delete. Surface gaps to negotiate with framework before scripts land.
Status: live
Created: 2026-05-01T02:18:11.531Z

### c-moma9qyz-682a80b0c4fa [live]
> Reshape the laboratory plugin from no-op stub to apparatus skeleton — package structure, kit-contribution exports, plugin metadata, TS types module. Foundation that subsequent children build on.
Status: live
Created: 2026-05-01T02:18:13.067Z

### c-moma9sg5-d42c5fc4e161 [live]
> Define the trial writ type and 'ext.laboratory.config' schema — TS types + JSON Schema, registered as a writ type in the lab's kit contribution. Schema mirrors the existing run.yaml shape from experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-artifacts.md.
Status: live
Created: 2026-05-01T02:18:14.981Z

### c-moma9ty6-521ce30ef1e8 [live]
> Author manifest YAML format and the 'nsg lab trial post --manifest <file>' CLI — manifest schema (same shape as ext.laboratory.config plus optional 'description' field), parser, validator, writ-post via existing CLI surfaces.
Status: live
Created: 2026-05-01T02:18:16.926Z

### c-moma9vrm-3af09f40c8fd [live]
> Author the post-and-collect-default rig template — composes fixture-setup, scenario, probe, teardown, and archive engines from the writ's ext.laboratory.config. Topological sort of fixture deps happens at template instantiation; rig itself stays a flat sequence.
Status: live
Created: 2026-05-01T02:18:19.282Z

### c-moma9y1k-5b14911925ba [live]
> Implement codex fixture engines (setup + teardown) — clone upstream at base SHA, push to GH as private repo with conventional name 'experiment-<X-num>-<slug>-run-<NNN>-<variant>'; teardown does 'gh repo delete'. Fail-loud safety check on teardown if the repo has unmerged content not in the archive.
Status: live
Created: 2026-05-01T02:18:22.232Z

### c-momaa03d-483afd0daa8b [live]
> Implement guild fixture engines (setup + teardown) — 'nsg init', 'nsg plugin install' per pin, 'nsg codex add', deep-merge guild.config, copy files; teardown rm -rfs the guild dir. Refuses to overwrite an existing guild dir on setup; refuses to teardown if archive hasn't run.
Status: live
Created: 2026-05-01T02:18:24.889Z

### c-momaa1vt-d6c8e41390a3 [live]
> Implement cross-guild scenario engines — 'commission-post-xguild' (shells out to 'nsg commission-post --guild-root <X>') and 'wait-for-writ-terminal-xguild' (polls writ status across guilds via --guild-root). Sequential commission posting only in v1; concurrent posting deferred.
Status: live
Created: 2026-05-01T02:18:27.209Z

### c-momaa3w7-41531f104086 [live]
> Implement standard probe engines — 'probe-stacks-dump' (exports test guild's books to JSON-per-table with carve-out for >50KB rows) and 'probe-git-range' (captures commit range between base and head SHAs as per-commit diffs + commits-manifest.yaml).
Status: live
Created: 2026-05-01T02:18:29.815Z

### c-momaa5o9-62fbf19d185d [live]
> Design and implement the archive engine — open sub-question on output target (lab guild books vs sanctum mirror vs hybrid?), per-trial storage layout, linkage from trial writ to archive artifacts. Design conclusion lands in this click; implementation follows in same scope.
Status: live
Created: 2026-05-01T02:18:32.121Z

### c-momaa75l-b5bca5260b9e [live]
> Smoke test — run a synthetic trial end-to-end through the apparatus (trivial brief, minimal plugin set) to validate the pipeline before porting real experiments. Lives in the laboratory plugin's tests.
Status: live
Created: 2026-05-01T02:18:34.041Z

### c-momaa8mk-26cdc988ce64 [live]
> Retire and migrate experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-artifacts.md — the apparatus replaces the spec's standalone bash scripts. Decide: delete the spec doc, archive it as historical, or rewrite it as a usage guide pointing at the apparatus. Conclude c-molveg89 in the same step.
Status: live
Created: 2026-05-01T02:18:35.948Z

### c-momaaa3t-75818b0a84d5 [live]
> Documentation — manifest authoring guide, laboratory plugin overview for /workspace/nexus/docs/architecture/, end-user how-to for evaluating prompts/plugins via trials. Audiences: nexus dev (architecture), end users (plugin docs).
Status: live
Created: 2026-05-01T02:18:37.865Z

### c-momaab8y-162fe87eb1fe [live]
> Port the first real-world trial — pick whichever P3 experiment is next (likely X016 orientation suppression), author its manifest, run it through the apparatus end-to-end. Validates the apparatus on real research work and produces the first real findings via the new pipeline.
Status: live
Created: 2026-05-01T02:18:39.346Z

## c-momaacry-90c54d5b79fa [parked]
> v2: introduce 'experiment' as a grouping concept above trial writs — when comparative views (cross-trial dashboards, integrating verdict, multi-variant analysis) earn their keep. Likely a new writ type that owns child trial writs, or a separate 'studies' book. Hold until comparative-analysis features have a motivating need.
Status: parked
Created: 2026-05-01T02:18:41.326Z

One experiment fans out to N trial writs. Conflating them would mash 'apparatus completed' with 'hypothesis confirmed' (a successful experiment can reject its hypothesis). Trial writ name still TBD — child click.
Created: 2026-04-30T22:05:40.190Z
Resolved: 2026-04-30T22:05:54.414Z

### c-mom19i4c-d8f6ff956216 [concluded]
> What's the writ-type name for one trial of an experiment? Bare 'run' is generic; want experiment-flavored (e.g., 'trial', 'assay', 'sortie', 'session-of-trial', etc.).
Status: concluded
Conclusion: Trial. Singular concept for MVP0 — one trial writ = one apparatus execution. 'Experiment' reserved for a future grouping concept (parent over multiple trials), introduced additively when comparative-analysis features earn their keep. Plain English across domains (drug trials, beta trials, trial runs); reads naturally for both A/B-test and research-style users.
Created: 2026-04-30T22:06:05.052Z
Resolved: 2026-05-01T02:07:07.196Z

## c-mom18zkt-9a0653e77a86 [concluded]
> Where do experiment-generated data live — in temp/test guilds, sanctum, or the lab-host guild?
Status: concluded
Conclusion: All experiment-generated data lives in the 'real' guild that hosts the Laboratory apparatus. Temp test-guilds are pure observation surfaces — set up, poked at, dumped, destroyed — never authoritative storage. Sanctum still holds methodology/inputs/exports for the research record; the lab-guild's books are the runtime source of truth.
Created: 2026-04-30T22:05:41.021Z
Resolved: 2026-04-30T22:05:57.857Z

## c-mom1907v-132b771959c7 [concluded]
> Reuse the 'Laboratory' name for the new apparatus, or pick a fresh one to avoid confusion with the retired data-mirroring plugin?
Status: concluded
Conclusion: Keep 'Laboratory.' The retired plugin's meaning was internal to Sean+Coco — no users carry mental models of it. The name is the clearest signal of intent for future users of the apparatus.
Created: 2026-04-30T22:05:41.851Z
Resolved: 2026-04-30T22:05:59.717Z

## c-mom19117-73866ec9da2b [concluded]
> Decompose 'fixture' into Fixture (lifecycle: setup/teardown) + Probe (data extraction), or keep the operations fused on a single concept?
Status: concluded
Conclusion: Split. Fixtures handle setup/teardown with an explicit dep DAG; probes extract data from one or more fixtures and run independently of the fixture lifecycle. Enables multi-probe-per-fixture (mid-trial diagnostic dump + end-of-trial archive dump), probe reuse across fixture types, and zero-probe fixtures (nexus-branch is one).
Created: 2026-04-30T22:05:42.907Z
Resolved: 2026-04-30T22:06:02.887Z

## c-mom19282-02f03d14a178 [concluded]
> Fixture scope/lifetime — trial-scoped (fresh per trial) vs experiment-scoped (shared across trials) vs ad-hoc? How is scope declared and enforced, and does it interact with the dep DAG (e.g., a trial-scoped fixture depending on an experiment-scoped one)?
Status: concluded
Conclusion: MVP0 ships trial-scope only — every fixture is fresh per trial, torn down at trial end. Reserve hooks for v2 without implementing: 'scope' field on fixture declarations (only 'trial' valid in v1), 'mutability' field ('mutable | read-only | snapshotted', informational in v1), DAG validator stub aware of 'lab > experiment > trial' ordering. Validation rule for the DAG: scope(B) >= scope(A) for every edge A->B (a dependency must outlive its dependent). Defer experiment- and lab-scope until a motivating use case appears (likely P3 with 20+ trials sharing a codex base SHA where re-cloning dominates cost).
Created: 2026-04-30T22:05:44.450Z
Resolved: 2026-05-01T02:12:22.861Z

## c-mom193ka-9aaac76e0e97 [concluded]
> How do we declaratively express the scenario — the workload that runs inside a trial after setup (commissions to post, ordering, end-conditions like queue-drains/timeout/rig-completes)? The spec doc currently assumes a human posts commissions manually; a real toolkit needs a scenario primitive.
Status: concluded
Conclusion: Scenario IS a rig template, dispatched via existing Spider/Reckoner machinery. No separate scenario primitive. MVP0 ships one canonical template (post-and-collect-default) covering the common shape (post N briefs sequentially, wait for terminal, run standard probes, archive). No in-template extension mechanism (no slots/graft); extension is via plugin-contributed templates. Friction-by-default avoids template proliferation; slot mechanics enter only when 3+ templates share most structure.
Created: 2026-04-30T22:05:46.186Z
Resolved: 2026-05-01T02:07:10.894Z

## c-mom9vkls-d395c6a934d1 [concluded]
> How is trial config carried into the rig — writ body, ext, or both?
Status: concluded
Conclusion: ext.laboratory.config carries the structured trial config (fixtures, plugin pins, files, brief refs, probe selections). Body stays free-form prose for human description (variant intent, hypothesis, notes). Authoring surface is a YAML manifest (same shape as existing run.yaml in experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-artifacts.md); CLI 'nsg lab trial post --manifest <file>' validates against the laboratory schema and posts the writ. Manifest format and writ ext are the same shape with a thin CLI translation — collapses two formats into one schema.
Created: 2026-05-01T02:07:11.632Z
Resolved: 2026-05-01T02:07:21.129Z

Status: parked

1	# The Laboratory (retired)
2	
3	Retired 2026-04-30. The Laboratory was an observational apparatus that watched Stacks CDC events on the Clerk's writs and links books and the Animator's sessions book, mirroring observational data into the sanctum at `experiments/data/commission-log.yaml` and `experiments/data/commissions/<id>/`. Its purpose was to feed the X013 (Commission Outcomes) research instrument with patron-subjective judgments alongside auto-collected telemetry.
4	
5	## Why it was retired
6	
7	Two pieces of the surrounding system shifted in ways that hollowed out the instrument:
8	
9	- **Spec generation became automated.** The Astrolabe planning pipeline now produces every commission's spec from a brief. Spec quality no longer varies along a patron-craft axis, so `spec_quality_pre/post` ratings are constant by construction.
10	- **Structured patron review was retired.** The patron stopped routinely filling in `complexity`, `outcome`, `failure_mode`, and `reviewed_at`. Per-commission review became ad-hoc as the planning workshop and static implement→review→revise rig pipeline absorbed the quality-assurance role.
11	
12	Without those signals, what remained in the commission log and per-commission directories was strictly mechanical — auto-set outcome (success/abandoned), the writ body as `commission.md`, and session telemetry — all of which is fully reproducible from the guild's own books.
13	
14	X013 was moved to Superseded; the Laboratory's three CDC watchers and helper code were deleted; this package was reduced to a no-op stub so existing guild.json registrations stay loadable.
15	
16	## Where the data lives now
17	
18	| Signal | Source |
19	|---|---|
20	| Writ status and lifecycle | `clerk/writs` book — query via `nsg writ show` / `nsg writ list` / `nsg writ tree` |
21	| Writ relationships (`fixes`, `depends-on`, etc.) | `clerk/links` book — query via `nsg writ` link tools |
22	| Session telemetry (cost, tokens, duration, exit, output) | `animator/sessions` book — query via `nsg session show` / `nsg session list` |
23	| Anima role and engine id per session | `animator/sessions` book — `metadata.role`, `metadata.engineId` |
24	| Engine→session linkage within a rig | `spider/rigs` book — query via `nsg rig for-writ` / `nsg rig show` |
25	
26	## Historical baseline
27	
28	The pruned commission log (150 patron-touched entries spanning 2026-03-25 → 2026-04-29) is preserved as an artifact in the experiments that reference it:
29	
30	- `experiments/X013-commission-outcomes/artifacts/2026-04-30-commission-log-frozen-baseline.yaml` — owned by the experiment that produced it.
31	- `experiments/X008-patrons-hands/artifacts/2026-04-30-commission-log-frozen-baseline.yaml` — referenced by §Infrastructure Milestones for the H5 review-rate-cliff evidence.
32	
33	The 22 commission directories (out of 1246) that contained substantive patron-written `review.md` notes remain at `experiments/data/commissions/<id>/`. The other 1224 — pure auto-generated content — were deleted.
34	
35	## Patron-side cleanup (non-urgent)
36	
37	The plugin is currently a no-op. Once you are ready to fully retire the package:
38	
39	1. Remove the `"laboratory"` entry from `/workspace/vibers/guild.json` plugins list.
40	2. Remove `@shardworks/laboratory-apparatus` from `/workspace/vibers/package.json` dependencies.
41	3. Restart the guild.
42	4. Delete `packages/laboratory/` from the sanctum.

file.content:
# The Laboratory (retired)

Retired 2026-04-30. The Laboratory was an observational apparatus that watched Stacks CDC events on the Clerk's writs and links books and the Animator's sessions book, mirroring observational data into the sanctum at `experiments/data/commission-log.yaml` and `experiments/data/commissions/<id>/`. Its purpose was to feed the X013 (Commission Outcomes) research instrument with patron-subjective judgments alongside auto-collected telemetry.

## Why it was retired

Two pieces of the surrounding system shifted in ways that hollowed out the instrument:

- **Spec generation became automated.** The Astrolabe planning pipeline now produces every commission's spec from a brief. Spec quality no longer varies along a patron-craft axis, so `spec_quality_pre/post` ratings are constant by construction.
- **Structured patron review was retired.** The patron stopped routinely filling in `complexity`, `outcome`, `failure_mode`, and `reviewed_at`. Per-commission review became ad-hoc as the planning workshop and static implement→review→revise rig pipeline absorbed the quality-assurance role.

Without those signals, what remained in the commission log and per-commission directories was strictly mechanical — auto-set outcome (success/abandoned), the writ body as `commission.md`, and session telemetry — all of which is fully reproducible from the guild's own books.

X013 was moved to Superseded; the Laboratory's three CDC watchers and helper code were deleted; this package was reduced to a no-op stub so existing guild.json registrations stay loadable.

## Where the data lives now

| Signal | Source |
|---|---|
| Writ status and lifecycle | `clerk/writs` book — query via `nsg writ show` / `nsg writ list` / `nsg writ tree` |
| Writ relationships (`fixes`, `depends-on`, etc.) | `clerk/links` book — query via `nsg writ` link tools |
| Session telemetry (cost, tokens, duration, exit, output) | `animator/sessions` book — query via `nsg session show` / `nsg session list` |
| Anima role and engine id per session | `animator/sessions` book — `metadata.role`, `metadata.engineId` |
| Engine→session linkage within a rig | `spider/rigs` book — query via `nsg rig for-writ` / `nsg rig show` |

## Historical baseline

The pruned commission log (150 patron-touched entries spanning 2026-03-25 → 2026-04-29) is preserved as an artifact in the experiments that reference it:

- `experiments/X013-commission-outcomes/artifacts/2026-04-30-commission-log-frozen-baseline.yaml` — owned by the experiment that produced it.
- `experiments/X008-patrons-hands/artifacts/2026-04-30-commission-log-frozen-baseline.yaml` — referenced by §Infrastructure Milestones for the H5 review-rate-cliff evidence.

The 22 commission directories (out of 1246) that contained substantive patron-written `review.md` notes remain at `experiments/data/commissions/<id>/`. The other 1224 — pure auto-generated content — were deleted.

## Patron-side cleanup (non-urgent)

The plugin is currently a no-op. Once you are ready to fully retire the package:

1. Remove the `"laboratory"` entry from `/workspace/vibers/guild.json` plugins list.
2. Remove `@shardworks/laboratory-apparatus` from `/workspace/vibers/package.json` dependencies.
3. Restart the guild.
4. Delete `packages/laboratory/` from the sanctum.

1	/**
2	 * @shardworks/laboratory-apparatus — The Laboratory (retired).
3	 *
4	 * This plugin is a retired observational apparatus. It previously watched
5	 * Stacks CDC events on the Clerk's writs and links books and the
6	 * Animator's sessions book, mirroring observational data into a sanctum
7	 * filesystem tree. As of 2026-04-30, the underlying research instrument
8	 * (the commission log) was deprecated — spec generation is now automated
9	 * (no patron-set quality variance), and structured patron review has been
10	 * retired. All collection points have been removed. The historical
11	 * commission-log baseline is preserved as an artifact under X013 and X008.
12	 *
13	 * The data the Laboratory used to mirror is fully available from the
14	 * guild books: writ status and links from `clerk` (writs, links), and
15	 * session telemetry (cost, tokens, duration, anima identity, engine id)
16	 * from `animator/sessions` plus structural engine-to-session linkage from
17	 * `spider/rigs`.
18	 *
19	 * The plugin is left as a no-op so existing guild.json registrations
20	 * remain loadable. Once the plugin entry is removed from each guild's
21	 * configuration, this package can be deleted entirely.
22	 */
23	
24	import type { Plugin } from '@shardworks/nexus-core';
25	
26	// Re-export the (now nearly-empty) config type for backward compatibility
27	// with any tooling that imports from this package.
28	export type { LaboratoryConfig } from './types.ts';
29	
30	const laboratoryPlugin: Plugin = {
31	  apparatus: {
32	    requires: [],
33	
34	    start: async () => {
35	      // No-op. This apparatus has been retired; all CDC watchers and
36	      // file-mirroring behavior have been removed.
37	    },
38	  },
39	};
40	
41	export default laboratoryPlugin;
42

1	/**
2	 * The Laboratory — configuration types (retired plugin).
3	 *
4	 * This file retains only the GuildConfig augmentation so that existing
5	 * guild.json files with a `laboratory` config block continue to typecheck.
6	 * The plugin itself is a no-op (see ./index.ts).
7	 */
8	
9	// ── Plugin config (no-op; retained for guild.json compatibility) ─────
10	
11	export interface LaboratoryConfig {
12	  /**
13	   * Path to the sanctum root directory. No longer used — the plugin is
14	   * retired. Field retained for backward-compatible guild.json parsing.
15	   */
16	  sanctumHome?: string;
17	
18	  /**
19	   * Path to the commissions data directory (no longer used).
20	   */
21	  commissionsDataDir?: string;
22	
23	  /**
24	   * Path to the commission log YAML file (no longer used).
25	   */
26	  commissionLogPath?: string;
27	}
28	
29	// Augment GuildConfig so `guild().guildConfig().laboratory` is typed.
30	declare module '@shardworks/nexus-core' {
31	  interface GuildConfig {
32	    laboratory?: LaboratoryConfig;
33	  }
34	}
